steps.title: Identify the required relationship
steps.content: The assertion predicts that flies from warmer months should have larger body sizes, which correlate with longer life spans. To support this claim, the data must show a warmer month with larger wing measurements than a cooler month.

steps.title: Find the data that confirms the prediction
steps.content:
Choice A shows that July, the warmer month (average high 87°F), had a larger average male wing centroid size (2.02 mm) than May, the cooler month (average high 73°F, 1.98 mm)—confirming the predicted relationship between warmth and body size.

The other choices either compare sexes rather than seasons (B), omit body size measurements entirely (C), or misstate values while presenting a temperature pattern that contradicts the assertion (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
